metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3 — g2p PAT secret handling**: specify that the org PAT for `gerrit_to_platform.ini` is stored as an SSM SecureString (e.g. `/rebar/prod/g2p-org-pat`) and materialized into the ini at boot/apply time, following the same pattern as `materialize-deploy-key.sh`; add the SSM parameter slot to `ssm.tf`.
findings.finding: The `gerrit_to_platform.ini` org PAT storage mechanism is unspecified — the plan says 'write gerrit_to_platform.ini (org PAT: Actions r/w, Contents+Metadata r)' but does not state whether the PAT value is injected from SSM or written as a plaintext ini file on the Gerrit host filesystem. Every other secret in this infra uses SSM SecureString + a materialize-at-boot script (gerrit-bot-token, github-replication-deploy-key, etc.); a plaintext PAT in an ini file on disk would break the established no-plaintext-secrets constraint.
findings.location: S3 AC — 'write gerrit_to_platform.ini (org PAT: Actions r/w, Contents+Metadata r)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `/rebar/prod/g2p-org-pat` to `local.rebar_secret_params` in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`. Write a `materialize-g2p-ini.sh` that reads the PAT from SSM and writes `gerrit_to_platform.ini` with 0600 permissions, analogous to `materialize-deploy-key.sh`. Reference this script from the g2p install runbook in S3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3**: Specify that the g2p PAT is stored in SSM SecureString (e.g. `/rebar/prod/g2p-github-pat`) and materialized into `gerrit_to_platform.ini` at startup by `fetch-secrets.sh` (or a dedicated script mirroring `materialize-deploy-key.sh`), consistent with the existing secret-store pattern.
findings.finding: The g2p PAT stored in gerrit_to_platform.ini is not specified to be SSM-backed or otherwise managed via the existing secret-store pattern (SSM SecureString + fetch-secrets.sh), leaving it as an unspecified plaintext file on the Gerrit host disk — inconsistent with every other sensitive credential in the infra (bot token, deploy key, GitHub OAuth secret) which all flow through SSM.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install AC: 'write gerrit_to_platform.ini (org PAT: Actions r/w, Contents+Metadata r)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new SSM SecureString slot `/rebar/prod/g2p-github-pat` (provisioned in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`); extend `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` to pull it and write it into `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (mode 0600) at boot, analogous to how the bot token and deploy key are handled. Reference this in S3's AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the Gerrit `hooks` plugin's bundle status in `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1`, add its download URL + SHA256 to `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`, extend `install-plugins.sh` if it requires a separate download, and document its coexistence contract with the already-deployed `webhooks` plugin before implementing S3.
findings.finding: The plan's S3 asserts 'enable the `hooks` (+ `replication`) plugins' as part of the g2p install, treating the Gerrit `hooks` plugin as a pre-existing or easily-enableable component. No evidence of this plugin exists anywhere in the codebase: `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` installs only `events-log.jar` and `oauth.jar`; `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` lists only `webhooks` (bundled), `events-log`, and `gerrit-oauth-provider`; no search for 'hooks plugin' in infra/ returns any result. The Gerrit `hooks` plugin (server-side hook scripts, distinct from the `webhooks` HTTP-delivery plugin) is the mechanism g2p uses to fire `workflow_dispatch` on `patchset-created`/`comment-added`/`change-merged` events. Its bundle status in the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image, its jar download source, its SHA256, and its interaction with the already-deployed `webhooks` plugin are all unverified by the plan.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install AC / 'enable the `hooks` (+ `replication`) plugins'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the Gerrit `hooks` plugin to `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` (download URL + sha256 pin + idempotent install, matching the existing `events-log`/`oauth` discipline) and record provenance in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`.
findings.finding: S3 says to 'enable the `hooks` (+ `replication`) plugins' on the Gerrit host, but `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` and `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` — the repo's sole plugin-installation artifacts — do not list the `hooks` plugin. Neither file is named as an edit target in S3. The `hooks` plugin (server-side script executor, distinct from the already-installed `webhooks` plugin) must be downloaded, sha256-pinned, and installed via `install-plugins.sh`, following the same discipline as `events-log` and `oauth`.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install AC; `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh`; `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `install_plugin "hooks.jar" "$HOOKS_URL" "$HOOKS_SHA256"` call to `install-plugins.sh`, pinning the artifact from `https://gerrit-ci.gerritforge.com/job/plugin-hooks-bazel-master-stable-3.14/lastSuccessfulBuild/artifact/bazel-bin/plugins/hooks/hooks.jar` (verify sha256 at pin time). Record the pin in `plugins/README.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **Observability gap — CI Verified voter**: add a CloudWatch alarm (a new `monitoring_s_ci.tf` following the `monitoring_s4b.tf` pattern) that fires when the host observability probe detects g2p hook dispatch failures or CI `Verified` vote failures, and wire it to the existing `aws_sns_topic.alerts`.
findings.finding: The plan adds no CloudWatch alarm or custom metric for g2p hook dispatcher failures or CI `Verified` vote failures. The established pattern for every new vote path adds a `monitoring_sX.tf` CloudWatch alarm (e.g. `monitoring_s4b.tf` alarms on `voter_errors` for LLM-Review failures); a silent g2p failure or SSH vote failure leaves changes permanently unsubmittable with no operator alert.
findings.location: S6 — E2E proof + docs / Locked design — Fail-closed section / no monitoring story

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3**: Confirm the Gerrit `hooks` plugin bundle status in the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image and add it to `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` with its enablement method (e.g. verifying the relevant `gerrit.config` `[hooks]` section or plugin list), so the S3 operator runbook is complete.
findings.finding: The plan describes g2p as installed via `pip install gerrit-to-platform` with console-script hooks symlinked into `$site/hooks/`, implying g2p runs as a Python package whose entry-point executables act as Gerrit `hooks` plugin scripts. This requires the Gerrit `hooks` plugin to be present and enabled. The existing `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` lists only `webhooks`, `events-log`, and `oauth` — the `hooks` plugin is NOT listed, and its bundle status in the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image is unconfirmed in any local file. While the Gerrit `hooks` plugin is bundled in standard Gerrit distributions, the plan's S3 does not name it for installation/enablement verification alongside the already-documented plugins.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install ('enable the `hooks` (+ `replication`) plugins'); infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md
findings.suggested_fix: Run `docker run --rm gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 ls /var/gerrit/plugins/` or inspect the image's bundled plugin list to confirm `hooks.jar` is present. If bundled, add a `hooks` row to `plugins/README.md` with 'bundled — enable via gerrit.config [plugins] install = hooks' (if not auto-enabled). If not bundled, add a download step to `install-plugins.sh` with a pinned sha256, following the same discipline as `events-log`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add observability and error-surfacing for the g2p hook→workflow_dispatch path: specify that g2p hook stderr is captured to a log file with rotation, define what happens on workflow_dispatch failure (does g2p retry? how many times?), and add a Gerrit change comment fallback (e.g., post a 'CI dispatch failed — recheck to retry' comment) so developers are not left with a silent missing vote.
findings.finding: The g2p hook→workflow_dispatch failure path (network error, GitHub API rate limit, mis-configured PAT) has no retry, no alerting, and no developer-visible feedback — the workflow is simply never dispatched, the developer sees no CI run and no vote, and the fail-closed guarantee is maintained only by silence rather than a surfaced error.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install / 'Verify coexistence with the existing review-bot webhook path'; Locked design — 'Fail-closed' section
findings.suggested_fix: In S3, specify: (1) g2p hook stderr redirected to a named log file (e.g., `$site/logs/gerrit_to_platform.log`) with logrotate; (2) configure g2p's `retry_count`/`retry_delay` in `gerrit_to_platform.ini` if supported, or wrap the hook in a shell script that retries dispatch up to 3× with exponential back-off; (3) if all retries fail, have the hook SSH into Gerrit and post a -1 Verified with message 'CI dispatch failed — post recheck to retry', so the failure is visible rather than silent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` as an explicit edit target in S1 and S2 (update the gate diagram/description and the replication refspec table respectively).
findings.finding: `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` is an unenumerated consumer: it documents the replication contract (including the explicit `refs/heads/main + refs/tags/*` refspec table, ADR-0010 rationale, and the gate design) and the project gate. S2 (replication broadening to `refs/*`) and S1 (new `Verified` label + updated submit requirement) will both make substantial portions of that doc stale, but `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` is not listed as an edit target in any story.
findings.location: S2 AC; S1 AC; `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task (in S3 or S6) to create a new ADR covering: (a) g2p adoption rationale vs. Jenkins/Zuul/bespoke, (b) the inbound SSH trust model (service account, key rotation policy, blast-radius), and (c) the two-vote gate contract — distinct from the ADR-0010 reversal ADR in S2.
findings.finding: The plan adopts g2p wholesale, opens an inbound SSH trust path from GitHub Actions to Gerrit (port 29418), and creates a new service account with Verified-vote ACL — together constituting a significant architectural decision about CI trust topology. Only the ADR-0010 reversal is explicitly scheduled for an ADR; the g2p adoption decision itself and the inbound SSH trust model are captured only in the plan's prose Decision log, not in a durable ADR that future operators can discover.
findings.location: Decision log; S3 — g2p install; S4 — Service account + credentials

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend S2 (or add an explicit S2a) to require a new ADR (e.g., ADR-0014) capturing: (a) adoption of g2p as the Gerrit→GHA bridge and the reasons Jenkins/Zuul/bespoke were rejected, (b) the inbound GitHub→Gerrit SSH vote path and its accepted blast-radius, and (c) the two-label gate topology — not just the ADR-0010 replication reversal.
findings.finding: The novel architectural decisions introduced by this epic — g2p adoption as the Gerrit→GitHub Actions bridge, and the inbound GitHub→Gerrit SSH vote path — are captured only in the plan's Decision log (ephemeral) and not committed to a repo ADR, leaving future maintainers without a durable rationale for these two foundational choices.
findings.location: Decision log (2026-07-01 brainstorm); S2 (ADR-0010 reversal only); S6 'capture design notes'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define an explicit emergency rollback path for the two-vote gate: specify the exact project.config change to temporarily revert to single-vote (LLM-Review only), who is authorized to execute it, and how in-flight changes at cutover receive their initial Verified vote (e.g., a one-time manual vote by the service account).
findings.finding: The gate change in S1 (adding `label:Verified=MAX` to the submit requirement) will immediately block all in-flight changes that already have `LLM-Review=+1` but have not yet been CI-verified, with no transition plan. There is no back-out procedure if the two-vote gate proves operationally disruptive (e.g., g2p is broken, the service account key is lost, or the CI is persistently flaky, leaving the repo ungatable).
findings.location: S1 — Gate config (infra/gerrit/project.config); Success Criteria 'The submit requirement requires BOTH votes'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback / Emergency bypass' sub-section to S1: 'To revert to single-vote gate, remove the [submit-requirement "Verified"] block and the AND label:Verified=MAX clause from the combined requirement. This requires a project.config commit to refs/meta/config. Trigger condition: CI infrastructure is unavailable for >N hours.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T12 deploy-window gap**: Document and implement a coexistence strategy for the S1→S5 window (e.g., deploy the submit requirement only after S5 is operational, or temporarily grant the CI service account a manual +1 placeholder, or add a 'CI-optional' phase where Verified is `anyOf` rather than required until the bridge is proven live).
findings.finding: Between S1 going live (Verified label added to submit requirement) and S3–S5 completing (g2p bridge installed, CI service account operational), ALL changes are blocked from submitting because no automated agent can cast Verified+1. The plan specifies the dependency ordering but does not address this deployment window where the gate is enforced but the vote-caster does not yet exist.
findings.location: Decomposition / AC S1 and S3–S5
findings.suggested_fix: Deploy S1 in two commits: first add the [label "Verified"] block and ACL (so the label exists and CI can vote) but keep the submit requirement as LLM-Review=MAX only; then add the `submittableIf = label:Verified=MAX AND label:LLM-Review=MAX` clause only after S5 is verified live in S6.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T9 credential retirement**: Add to S4 a key-rotation runbook (generate new keypair → update GitHub secret → update Gerrit service account authorized_keys → verify CI voting resumes) and a decommission checklist (revoke Gerrit service account, delete GitHub secret, remove ACL grant).
findings.finding: The lifecycle of the GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY GitHub Actions secret and the corresponding Gerrit service account SSH key has no defined RETIRE or rotation path. The plan creates the credential in S4 but does not specify how it is rotated if compromised or when the PoC is decommissioned.
findings.location: AC S4 — Service account + credentials

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S5**: Before implementing the `clear-vote` job, verify that `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` actually accepts `vote-type: clear` (or the equivalent reset value) by inspecting the action's `action.yml` inputs definition and confirming the exact input name/value from the upstream source.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `gerrit-review-action` accepts `vote-type: clear` as an input to reset `Verified→0` at run start (the clear-vote job). This specific input value (`clear`) is central to the GerriScary-safe reset and is asserted as part of the g2p production pattern, but its existence as a named input value of `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` is UNVERIFIED against the action's actual `action.yml` interface — the plan cannot be run or the action's help inspected here.
findings.location: Locked design section — 'clear-vote → `gerrit-review-action vote-type: clear`'; AC S5 — 'clear-vote → `gerrit-review-action` vote-type: clear'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit task in S1 or S6 to update or supersede ADR-0013 to reflect the two-vote gate (LLM-Review + Verified), referencing the new ADR for the ADR-0010 reversal and the g2p integration decision.
findings.finding: ADR-0013 (LLM-Review label) documents the existing single-vote gate architecture. The plan changes the gate to require two independent votes but does not explicitly plan to update or supersede ADR-0013. This will leave ADR-0013 stale and potentially misleading to future contributors who read it as authoritative.
findings.location: S1 — Gate config; Key references ('ADR-0013 (LLM-Review label)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3 — g2p install environment**: Specify and verify where g2p is installed relative to the Dockerized Gerrit (host OS with bind-mounted `$site/hooks/`, custom image layer, or sidecar container), confirm Python 3.11+ is available in that environment, and document how the installation survives a container rebuild.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'pip install gerrit-to-platform (Py≥3.11); symlink the three console-script hooks into $site/hooks/' without addressing how Python 3.11+ is available inside a Dockerized Gerrit. The gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 image (confirmed in infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh and plugins/README.md) is a Java-only image with no Python runtime. The existing plugin install pattern (install-plugins.sh) uses shell+curl, not Python. If g2p hooks run inside the Gerrit container, they require a Python runtime that is not present in the base image; if they run on the host OS outside the container, $site/hooks/ is bind-mounted and Python on the host is sufficient — but the plan does not specify which path is taken, leaving the install surface ambiguous and the capability assertion unverified for this deployment topology.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install / Acceptance Criteria S3
findings.suggested_fix: The cleanest path for a Dockerized Gerrit is to install g2p on the host OS (the EC2 instance) and bind-mount or symlink `$site/hooks/` from the host path. Document this explicitly in S3: 'Install g2p on the host OS (`pip3 install gerrit-to-platform`) where the Gerrit site's `hooks/` directory is accessible (via the bind mount at `/var/gerrit/site`); symlink the console-script hooks from the host Python environment into the mounted path.' Alternatively, build a thin custom Gerrit image (`FROM gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1; RUN apt-get install -y python3.11 python3-pip && pip3 install gerrit-to-platform`) and update `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2**: Replace the plan's vague `refs/*` broadening with the precise refspec additions needed (e.g. `push = refs/changes/*:refs/changes/*`) and explicitly confirm that `refs/meta/*` remains excluded — reconciling with ADR-0014's dependency on `replicatePermissions = false`.
findings.finding: The plan's S2 states 'mirror `refs/*` (change refs) to the GitHub repo', but a literal `refs/*` push refspec would include `refs/meta/config` — the exact ref that ADR-0014 and ADR-0010 (`replicatePermissions = false`) are designed to keep off GitHub because it embeds the webhook token. The existing `replication.config` explicitly lists only `refs/heads/main` and `refs/tags/*` to avoid this. The broadening must be expressed as specific refspecs (e.g. `refs/changes/*:refs/changes/*` added alongside existing lines) rather than a wildcard `refs/*`, or `replicatePermissions` must remain false AND the refspec scoped to exclude `refs/meta/*`.
findings.location: S2 — Replication broadening; 'Locked design' section ('Broaden Gerrit replication to mirror `refs/*`'); infra/gerrit/replication.config; docs/adr/0010-gerrit-github-replication.md; docs/adr/0014-inbound-webhook-auth.md
findings.suggested_fix: In the updated `replication.config`, add `push = refs/changes/*:refs/changes/*` as an additional line alongside the existing `refs/heads/main` and `refs/tags/*` lines, and keep `replicatePermissions = false`. Do NOT use `refs/*:refs/*` or `mirror = true`. Update the ADR-0010 supersession to name the exact refspecs added and re-confirm the ADR-0014 dependency is preserved.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'Verify coexistence with the existing review-bot webhook path' in S3 with a concrete proving command: e.g. 'After install, push a test patchset and confirm (a) the review-bot posts an LLM-Review vote AND (b) a gerrit-verify workflow_dispatch is visible in GitHub Actions — both triggered from the same patchset-created hook event.'
findings.finding: S3's acceptance criterion 'Verify coexistence with the existing review-bot webhook path' provides no concrete proving command or observable outcome — it is a hedge without a test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — S3 (g2p install)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S5 (workflow spec):** Add an inline note defining the `gerrit-review-action` `vote-type` accepted vocabulary — specifically: confirm `clear` resets to `Verified=0` (not label removal), and confirm whether `vote-type` accepts raw `im-open/workflow-conclusion` strings or requires a pass-through conditional mapping; cite the upstream action's parameter schema.
findings.finding: The `vote-type` parameter vocabulary for `gerrit-review-action` is used with three distinct values in the plan (`clear`, the WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION string, and implicitly a numeric vote), but the plan never defines the complete accepted vocabulary of this action parameter or confirms whether it accepts raw `im-open/workflow-conclusion` output strings or requires a different enum. An executor implementing S5 cannot confirm the `vote-type: clear` semantics (resets to 0 vs. removes the vote entirely) or whether passing a raw conclusion string is valid input.
findings.location: Locked design — job 1 `clear-vote` and job 3 `vote` descriptions; S5 AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name an explicit provisioning artifact for the CI service account in S4 (e.g., a new `infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh` modeled on `service-user.sh`, or a documented extension of the existing script) so the SSH-key issuance, group assignment, and SSM/GitHub secret population are reproducible and auditable.
findings.finding: S4 creates a new CI Gerrit service account with an SSH key for casting `Verified` votes, but `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (the only existing service-account provisioning script) is not named as an edit target or template reference. The new CI account needs analogous provisioning (create account, assign to a group, rotate key into SSM/GitHub secret) and the plan provides no script-level artifact for this, leaving the executor to improvise.
findings.location: S4 AC — 'Service account + credentials'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S5 (vote job):** Define the complete `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` enum mapping — enumerate all values `im-open/workflow-conclusion` can emit (success, failure, cancelled, skipped, timed_out, neutral, action_required) and explicitly state which map to Verified +1, which to Verified -1, and which (if any) should be treated as -1 by the `gerrit-review-action` default; add a conditional or explicit `vote-type` mapping table co-located with the workflow spec.
findings.finding: The `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` enum produced by `im-open/workflow-conclusion` has more values than the plan's stated two-branch mapping (success / failure|cancelled). The plan says `gerrit-review-action vote-type: ${{ WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION }}` casts Verified +1 (success) or -1 (failure|cancelled), but `im-open/workflow-conclusion` can also emit `skipped`, `timed_out`, `neutral`, and `action_required`. The plan never specifies what Gerrit vote those values produce — whether `gerrit-review-action` maps them to -1, 0, or errors — leaving the executor to guess at critical gate behavior for edge-case run conclusions.
findings.location: Locked design section — job 3 `vote` description; S5 AC
findings.suggested_fix: In S5, add a mapping table: success→+1; all others (failure, cancelled, timed_out, skipped, neutral, action_required)→-1. Use an explicit `if: steps.conclusion.outputs.conclusion == 'success'` conditional or confirm via the g2p example workflows that `gerrit-review-action` already maps any non-success to -1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S4**: Document the procedure for obtaining and verifying the GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS value: specify that the Gerrit SSH host key fingerprint must be retrieved via a trusted, authenticated channel (e.g. AWS SSM Session Manager console on the host itself with `ssh-keyscan localhost 29418`) and cross-checked against the value stored in the repo variable before CI runs begin.
findings.finding: The plan names GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS as a GitHub repo variable but does not specify how the value is established — specifically, whether the Gerrit SSH host key fingerprint is verified out-of-band before being committed to the variable. If the workflow uses StrictHostKeyChecking=no or accepts the first-seen host key, the inbound vote path (GitHub runners → Gerrit:29418) is vulnerable to a MITM attack where a forged Gerrit server accepts the SSH connection and receives the CI service account's private key authentication attempt.
findings.location: S4 — Service account + credentials: 'set GitHub repo vars GERRIT_SERVER / GERRIT_SSH_USER / GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS / GERRIT_URL'
findings.suggested_fix: In the S4 runbook step, add: 'Run `ssh-keyscan -p 29418 <GERRIT_HOST>` from the Gerrit host via SSM Session Manager; compare the output fingerprint against the one registered at host provision time (stored in SSM or the runbook); only then set GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS in the GitHub repo variable. Never use StrictHostKeyChecking=no or auto-accept from a remote network location.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3**: Reduce the g2p PAT scope to `Actions: write` (dispatch) only, removing `Contents` and `Metadata` scopes; confirm with the g2p README that no Contents scope is needed for the dispatch-only path.
findings.finding: The g2p PAT scope 'Contents+Metadata r' grants read access to the full repository source tree, which is broader than what g2p requires (workflow dispatch + status read). Granting Contents:read to the bridge process is not least-privilege — g2p only needs to trigger and observe workflow runs via the Actions API.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install AC: 'org PAT: Actions r/w, Contents+Metadata r'
findings.suggested_fix: Use a fine-grained PAT scoped to `navapbc/rebar` with only `Actions: write` permission. If g2p's implementation requires Metadata:read (repo existence check), add that alone, but do not include Contents:read.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to S2's 'confirm the mirror-lock ruleset still bars non-replication writes' criterion — e.g. 'Attempt `git push origin HEAD:main` from a non-replication account on the GitHub mirror and assert the push is rejected by the branch ruleset (exit non-zero with a ruleset violation message).'
findings.finding: S2's acceptance criterion says 'confirm the mirror-lock ruleset still bars non-replication writes to main' but specifies no proving command — an executor cannot demonstrate this without guessing at the verification method.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — S2 (Replication broadening)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Close the test.yml convergence decision in S5: either record 'we WILL use the gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml hybrid (single file)' or 'we WILL keep two separate files' as a closed statement, and rewrite the AC as an observable outcome (e.g. 'A single `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` exists that is triggered by both `workflow_dispatch` from g2p and direct `push`/`pull_request` events, replacing the standalone `test.yml`').
findings.finding: S5's acceptance criterion says 'Decide test.yml convergence (recommend the gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml hybrid…)' — deferring a real file-architecture decision to the executor rather than recording a closed decision.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — S5 (The workflow)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Decide test.yml convergence (recommend the gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml hybrid…)' with 'The workflow file is `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`, structured as a gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch hybrid: triggered by `workflow_dispatch` (g2p) AND `push`/`pull_request` (branch CI), superseding `test.yml`. The old `test.yml` is deleted.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the refs/* replication broadening: specify how to revert replication.config to main+tags-only, what cleanup is needed for already-replicated refs/changes/* refs on GitHub, and under what conditions the rollback would be triggered.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges the ADR-0010 reversal (refs/* replication broadening) as a deliberate breaking change but provides no explicit rollback/back-out plan. Per T4, acknowledgement alone is insufficient — the remedy must include a reversibility mechanism. If the broadened replication causes problems (e.g., GitHub quota exhaustion from change refs, replication lag spikes, or an edge case in the mirror-lock ruleset), there is no defined procedure to revert to main+tags-only mirroring without leaving dangling refs.
findings.location: S2 — Replication broadening + superseding ADR; Decision log 'Two entailed posture changes accepted'
findings.suggested_fix: In S2, add a sub-step: 'Rollback: if broadened replication causes quota/lag/security issues, revert replication.config to `refs/heads/*` + `refs/tags/*`, delete any refs/changes/* refs from the GitHub remote via `git push --delete`, and re-enable the old pattern. Document the trigger conditions (e.g., >N replication errors/hour, GitHub API 429s, mirror-lock bypass detected).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T12 rollback path**: Add an explicit rollback procedure to S6 (or a dedicated ops runbook) specifying the exact `project.config` and `replication.config` edits to revert each posture change, and confirm the revert is a simple git push to `All-Projects` that takes effect without a Gerrit restart.
findings.finding: The plan has no explicit rollback procedure for the two major posture changes (refs/* replication broadening and the Verified submit requirement). It only notes 'the underlying ADRs are reconsiderable' without specifying what a fast revert looks like operationally.
findings.location: Locked design / Decision log / AC S2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3 — g2p install idempotency**: specify (or write) an idempotent `install-g2p.sh` script (following `setup-replication.sh` / `service-user.sh` patterns) that is safe to re-run: checks whether the correct version is already installed, creates symlinks with `ln -sf` (force, not fail-if-exists), and writes the ini atomically.
findings.finding: The g2p install steps in S3 (pip install, symlink hooks, write ini) are described as one-shot imperative commands with no stated idempotency guarantee or re-runnable script. The existing infra scripts are explicitly documented as idempotent and safe to re-run (e.g. service-user.sh: 'Idempotent + rotating: re-running creates the account if absent (else rotates its token)'); an imperative install with no idempotency story means re-running it after a partial failure is risky.
findings.location: S3 AC — 'pip install gerrit-to-platform (Py≥3.11); symlink the three console-script hooks into $site/hooks/; write gerrit_to_platform.ini'
findings.suggested_fix: Write `infra/gerrit/install-g2p.sh` with `set -euo pipefail`, a `DRY_RUN` flag, a version check (`pip show gerrit-to-platform | grep Version`), `ln -sf` for the three hook symlinks, and an atomic ini write (write to a temp file, then `mv`).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
